title: Section 26
content: 1[26. Decision to be by majority.--If the Members of a Bench differ in opinion on any point, the point shall be decided according to the opinion of the majority, if there is a majority, but if the Members are equally divided, they shall state the point or points on which they differ, and make a reference to the Chairman who shall either hear the point or points himself or refer the case for hearing on such point or points by one or more of the other Members of the Tribunal and such point or points shall be decided according to the opinion of the majority of the Members of the Tribunal who have heard the case, including those who first heard it.]
footnote: 1. Subs. by Act 19 of 1986, s. 17, for sections 25 and 26 (w.e.f. 22-1-1986).
